title: Cloud Run Revisions, Validation, Traffic, Rollback, and Recovery · Automation Case: Ci Traffic Split Canary Signals
summary: A pipeline deploys latest, routes all traffic before readiness and smoke checks, changes IAM with application release, cannot identify the prior digest, and calls redeployment a rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci traffic split canary signals increment in progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Move bounded traffic by revision or tag, define observation window, compare user and system signals, and stop on explicit abort conditions.
Validate revision readiness, configuration, identity, logs, authenticated smoke behavior, changed inputs, and dependency failure before traffic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Move bounded traffic by revision or tag, define observation window, compare user and system signals, and stop on explicit abort conditions. A defensible solution applies Ci Traffic Split Canary Signals, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-5
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-5
    artifact: cloud-run-delivery.yaml
    decision: worked cd cloud run progressive delivery 5
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      deploy-cloudrun-action: 3
      service: status-api
      region: us-central1
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      revision-suffix: run8842
      traffic: none-at-deploy
      smoke: authenticated-changed-case
      canary-percent: 5
      observe: latency-errors-user-success
      abort: explicit-threshold
      rollback: prior-revision-traffic
      iam: separately-owned
    verify: inspect revision image identity readiness authenticated smoke traffic totals observation window abort decision rollback and IAM audit
    failure: Reject latest tags, deploy acceptance as readiness, instant full traffic, representative-canary assumptions, rebuild rollback, or deployment-owned public IAM.
    repair: Deploy verified digest without traffic, smoke exact revision, canary with signals, abort predictably, move traffic back, and separate IAM.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
